title: Lower of Cost or Net Realizable Value
event: Hollis Co.'s inventory cost is $65,000. Estimated selling price is $70,000 and estimated costs to complete and sell are $8,000. Prepare the required entry (loss method).

lines.label: 65,000 − 62,000
lines.why: The loss method names the decline on its own line rather than burying it in COGS.
lines.trap: NRV = selling price MINUS completion/selling costs (70,000 − 8,000) — skipping the subtraction misses the write-down entirely.

lines.label: carried at NRV
lines.why: Inventory lands at 62,000, the lower figure.
lines.trap: Credit only the DECLINE; an allowance account is the common alternative credit.

memorize.body: NRV = estimated selling price − costs to complete and sell. Report the LOWER of cost or NRV.
COGS method buries the loss in cost of goods sold; loss method displays it separately. Same balance sheet either way.

questions.prompt: Cost $65,000, selling price $70,000, costs to sell $8,000. Write-down = ?

distractors.feedback: You compared cost to the 70,000 selling price. NRV nets the 8,000 selling costs first: 62,000 < 65,000 → write down 3,000.
That's selling price minus cost. The write-down is cost minus NRV: 65,000 − 62,000 = 3,000.

accountBank: Allowance to Reduce Inventory to NRV
build.scaffold: Compute NRV first (price − costs to sell), then write down only the gap below cost.
